title: Mathieu Griffi

[[Occitania (administrative region)|Occitania]], France |height = {{convert|187|cm|ftin|abbr=on}} |weight = {{convert|101|kg|stlb|abbr=on}} |club1 = [[Catalans Dragons]] |year1start = 2006 |year1end = 08 |appearances1 = 26 |tries1 = 0 |goals1 = 0 |fieldgoals1 = 0 |points1 = 0 |club2 = {{nowrap|[[Toulouse Olympique]]}} |year2start = 2009 |year2end = 10 |appearances2 = 18 |tries2 = 5 |goals2 = 0 |fieldgoals2 = 0 |points2 = 20 |club3 = [[Lézignan Sangliers]] |year3start = 2011 |year3end = |appearances3 = |tries3 = |goals3 = |fieldgoals3 = |points3 = |teamA = [[France national rugby league team|France]] |yearAstart = 2008 |yearAend = 10 |appearancesA = 3 |triesA = 0 |goalsA = 0 |fieldgoalsA = 0 |pointsA = 0 |updated = 21 September 2016 |source ={{cite web|url=https://www.loverugbyleague.com/stats/players/mathieu-griffi|title=Statistics at loverugbyleague.com|publisher=loverugbyleague.com|date=31 December 2019|access-date=1 January 2020}}{{cite web|url=http://www.rugbyleagueproject.org/players/mathieu-griffi/summary.html|title=Statistics at rugbyleagueproject.org|publisher=Rugby League Project|date=31 December 2019|access-date=1 January 2020}}{{cite web|url=http://www.itsrugby.fr/joueur_10751.html|title=Statistics at itsrugby.fr|publisher=itsrugby.fr|date=31 December 2019|access-date=1 January 2020}}{{cite web|url=http://championshipstats.rlfans.com/Teams/Toulouse-XIII/The-Co-operative-Championship-2009/index.html|title=Statistics at championshipstats.rlfans.com|publisher=championshipstats.rlfans.com|date=31 December 2019|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20100113013132/http://championshipstats.rlfans.com/Teams/Toulouse-XIII/The-Co-operative-Championship-2009/index.html|access-date=1 January 2020|archive-date=13 January 2010}} }} '''Mathieu Griffi''' (born 2 March 1983) is a French former professional [[rugby league]] footballer who played in the 2000s and 2010s. He played at representative level for [[France national rugby league team|France]], and at club level for the [[Catalans Dragons]], [[Toulouse Olympique]] and in the [[Elite One Championship]] for [[Lézignan Sangliers]], as a {{rlp|pr}}. ==Background== Mathieu Griffi was born in [[Aude]], France,{{citation needed|date=February 2020}} and he is of Italian descent.{{cite web|url=http://www.ffr13.fr/index.php/competitions/championnat-de-france/elite-1/index.php?option%3Dcom_content%26view%3Darticle%26id%3D1516 |title=Calegari l'Azzuri |access-date=2012-10-14 |url-status=dead |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20120616151500/http://www.ffr13.fr/index.php/competitions/championnat-de-france/elite-1/index.php?option=com_content&view=article&id=1516 |archive-date=16 June 2012 }} ==Playing career== He was named in the [[France national rugby league team#2008 World Cup Squad|France training squad]] for the [[2008 Rugby League World Cup]],{{cite web|url=http://www.skysports.com/story/0,19528,12196_3949078,00.html|title=France dominated by Dragons|publisher=Sky Sports|date=7 August 2008|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20080919010800/http://www.skysports.com/story/0,19528,12196_3949078,00.html|access-date=2008-08-08|archive-date=19 September 2008}} he was named in the [[2008 Rugby League World Cup squads#France|France squad]] for the [[2008 Rugby League World Cup]],{{cite web|url=http://www.rlwc08.com/news/news/archive/2008/09/24/france-name-official-squad.aspx |title=France name official squad |publisher=RLWC2008 |date=24 September 2008 |access-date=2008-10-03 |url-status=dead |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20080930072635/http://www.rlwc08.com/news/news/archive/2008/09/24/france-name-official-squad.aspx |archive-date=30 September 2008 }} and he also represented France in the [[2009 Four Nations]] tournament. ==References== {{reflist}} {{France 2008 Rugby League World Cup squad}} {{2009 France Rugby League Four Nations squad}} {{DEFAULTSORT:Griffi, Mathieu}}
